text: **Speaker 1:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**Speaker 1:** Jill.
**Speaker 1:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Speaker 1:** People around the world live in different {countries|国々|/ˈkʌntriz/|Many countries have unique cultures.}

**Speaker 1:** We eat different {foods|食べ物|/fuːdz/|I like trying different foods.} and speak different {languages|言語|/ˈlæŋɡwɪdʒɪz/|People speak many languages worldwide.}, but we share something big, a solar system.
**Speaker 1:** In English, solar means {sun|太陽|/sʌn/|The sun is very bright.}
**Speaker 1:** So a solar system is a sun with {objects|物体|/ˈɒbdʒɪkts/|Some objects float in space.} {orbiting|回る|/ˈɔːrbɪtɪŋ/|Planets are orbiting the sun.} around it.
**Speaker 1:** But what are those objects?
**Speaker 1:** What is in our solar system?
**Speaker 1:** Today, Anna helps us answer that question.
**Speaker 1:** What is in our solar system?
Hello!
Welcome to my treehouse
This is my new {Earth|地球|/ɜːrθ/|Earth is our home planet.} ball.
We live on planet Earth.
**Speaker 3:** Anna, our question today is, what is in our solar system?
There are eight {planets|惑星|/ˈplænɪts/|Planets move around the sun.} in our solar system, and some of those planets have {moons|月|/muːnz/|Moons orbit around planets.}.
**Speaker 3:** There are many moons in our solar system.
But there is only one sun.
**Speaker 3:** The sun rises in the east and sets in the west.
So the sun can help us find {directions|方向|/dəˈrekʃənz/|Directions help us navigate.}
**Speaker 3:** At night, {stars|星|/stɑːrz/|Stars shine brightly at night.} can help us find our way too.
There is no sun at night, and there are no stars in the day.
So I use my phone for directions.
Daisy, phones are great, but sometimes they do not work.
I do not lose my way, Anna.
You do.
You get lost a lot.
Daisy, you are
You are right.
She is {right|正しい/正確な}.
I {get lost|迷う/道に迷う} a lot.
**Speaker 1:** We all see the same {moon|月} at night and the same sun during the day.
**Speaker 1:** The Sun is a {star|星} at the center of our solar system.
**Speaker 1:** All the {planets|惑星} {rotate|回転する} or move around it.
**Speaker 1:** There are eight planets in our solar system.
**Speaker 1:** There were nine planets, but {Pluto|冥王星} is no longer a planet.
**Speaker 1:** Poor Pluto!
**Speaker 1:** Can you {name|名前を挙げる} all eight planets?
**Speaker 1:** Let's go back to Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She helps us.
This is our {solar system|太陽系}.
There are eight planets in our solar system.
Say them after me.
Are you ready?
Let's go.
Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
There is, there are.
We use there is to talk about one thing.
We use there are to talk about two or more things.
There are many {stars|星} in the night sky, but there is only one star in our solar system, the sun.
There are many things to see in space.
There is nothing to see in space.
{Daisy|デイジー}, let's use the {travel|旅行の} {walkie-talkies|トランシーバー} to go to space!
No!
Yes!
**Speaker 1:** What do you know about our solar system?
**Speaker 1:** Let's answer some questions about it.
Which planet is closest to the {Sun|太陽}?
Mercury
Which {planet|惑星/天体|/ˈplænɪt/|Many planets orbit the sun.} is third from the Sun?
Earth.
Which planet is {farthest|最も遠い|/ˈfɑːrðɪst/|Neptune is the farthest planet.} from the Sun?
Neptune.
**Speaker 1:** We talked about the Sun, {planets|惑星|/ˈplænɪts/|Planets rotate around the sun.}, and moons.
**Speaker 1:** But there are {comets|彗星|/ˈkɑːmɪts/|Comets have long tails.} and {asteroids|小惑星|/ˈæstərɔɪdz/|Asteroids are rocky objects.} in our solar system too.
**Speaker 1:** There are many {stars|星|/stɑːrz/|Stars shine brightly at night.} in the night sky.
**Speaker 1:** But they are not in our solar system.
**Speaker 1:** There is only one star, our sun.
Daisy, look!
Neptune!
Let's go {forward|前へ|/ˈfɔːrwərd/|Move forward slowly.}
Where are we?
And what is that?
I feel {hot|暑い/熱い|/hɑːt/|The sun makes me feel hot.}
It is the sun.
**Speaker 1:** Talking about our solar system is fun.
**Speaker 1:** It is even more fun watching Anna and Daisy {explore|探検する|/ɪkˈsplɔːr/|Explorers discover new places.} solar system.
**Speaker 1:** Practice using the words in this show to talk about our solar system.
**Speaker 1:** And join us again next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
